# CONCEPT 65 — ARCADE VOXEL WORLD / PIXEL-ASSEMBLY COUTURE (SILENT · GAME-TYPE)

Title: Pillar 7 — Pop-Surreal Digital

Format: Cinematic Beauty Transformation (AX — game-world category) — voxel pixel-assembly set transform — fully silent reel

Hero Product: "Pixel Pop" liquid highlighter stick — a glossy iridescent twist-up stick in a holographic case that shifts colour with the light

Setting: a bright stylized voxel game-world — blocky pixel landscape, floating platforms, glowing power-up blocks, candy-bright sky, drifting pixel-motes (clean, no on-screen text or HUD)

Wardrobe: a matte cobalt slip (start) → transforms via glowing voxel pixel-assembly into a structured digital-couture gown of neat luminous facets

Hair: High Glossy Pop Ponytail with Blunt Fringe

Makeup: Hyper-Pop Iridescent Glow (luminous skin, iridescent lid shimmer, graphic clean lash, glossy candy lip)

Movement: a confident turn → voxel pixel-assembly transform → product highlighter-stick draw hero

Concept Line (on-theme, NOT spoken): "level up"

Duration: 30 seconds | 6 frames

Mood: PLAYFUL electric → triumphant

Composition: centred hero in the voxel world, bold pop colour, clean negative space

Grade: hyper-pop candy-bright — electric blue, magenta, lime, sunny yellow, clean bright light (cinematic-bright lane, –3), no teal-orange

Spoken line: NONE — fully silent reel, no VO; visuals + chiptune-cinematic sound design carry it (Frame 4 is a wordless direct-to-lens "player-one" beauty beat)

Audio note: per-frame in-video AUDIO is diegetic-plus-design with no spoken word on any frame; a separate 🎵 Master Track Brief follows the final frame (low and subtle, never full).

Identity safety: the transform locks BOTH keyframes — start = pre-transform recognizable face + cobalt slip; end = voxel digital-couture gown, new AND clearly her; the pixel-assembly covers only the crossing; only the named transform is impossible, all light, voxel-glow and fabric obey believable physics within the stylized world.

Visual-lead note (silent reel): with zero dialogue, every beat is built to stop a scroll on image alone — bold pop colour, crisp voxel geometry, a commanding player-one hero look and a maximal power-up climax.

Keep expression changes smooth and moderate and lead them with the EYES and GAZE (soft smiles, brow lifts, a parted breath, eyes tracing the object) — no extreme grimace, no wide-mouth distortion, no hard squint, no rapid expression flicker that would warp the geometry; favour one clean expression transition per clip. ABSOLUTE ZERO identity drift: no morphing, melting, smearing, rubbery warping, face-swapping, twinning or a second/duplicate subject, no age, weight, ethnicity or gender shift, no extra, missing or fused fingers or limbs. Identity stays identical at the first frame, every in-between frame, the loop seam and the last frame. Through any transform, impossible move or fast action ONLY the intended element (wardrobe, environment, props or effect) changes — her face, identity and proportions stay fully recognizable; interpolate strictly between the locked keyframes and never invent a new face, new hands, extra limbs or a second subject. Do not mirror, flip or invert the frame.

## Frame 4 of 6 — VIDEO PROMPT (wordless beauty beat — no VO; visual-led)

IDENTITY & CHARACTER-CONSISTENCY LOCK: render Aira strictly from the locked identity reference and hold her the SAME PERSON for the entire 6-second clip and every generated frame. Treat identity as a FIXED skeleton that never changes — bone structure (skull, cheekbones, jawline, chin), overall face shape and proportions, eye shape, spacing and iris colour, nose and lip shape, skin tone and real skin texture (visible pores, fine peach-fuzz; no plastic smoothing, no beautification creep, no face re-shaping), hairline, hair colour and texture, height, build and hand/finger anatomy. ONLY the soft expression muscles may move — eyelids, brows, cheeks and mouth corners — together with the eyes, gaze and head; expression and lip-sync animate ON TOP of that fixed identity and must NEVER alter the face shape, the eye colour, or who she is. Keep expression changes smooth and moderate and lead them with the EYES and GAZE (soft smiles, brow lifts, a parted breath, eyes tracing the object) — no extreme grimace, no wide-mouth distortion, no hard squint, no rapid expression flicker that would warp the geometry; favour one clean expression transition per clip. ABSOLUTE ZERO identity drift: no morphing, melting, smearing, rubbery warping, face-swapping, twinning or a second/duplicate subject, no age, weight, ethnicity or gender shift, no extra, missing or fused fingers or limbs. Identity stays identical at the first frame, every in-between frame, the loop seam and the last frame. Through any transform, impossible move or fast action ONLY the intended element (wardrobe, environment, props or effect) changes — her face, identity and proportions stay fully recognizable; interpolate strictly between the locked keyframes and never invent a new face, new hands, extra limbs or a second subject. Do not mirror, flip or invert the frame.

SHOT TYPE: bold beauty close, chest-up, commanding wordless player-one address.

LENS: 85mm, f/2.5.

SHOT BREAKDOWN (timed, 6s · real-time, continuous energetic motion — never slow-motion, never a static hold; expression eye-led and identity-safe):

- [00:04–00:06] The smile holds, eyes direct and bright — a wordless player-one expression reveal in place of any line.

VO — none (the beat is carried by the look, not a line). Master track sits low and steady under the beat (subtle, never full).

DURATION: 6 seconds (the clip plays the full 6s at real-time natural speed).

FRAME RATE + MOTION BLUR: 24fps, real-time playback at natural speed (no slow-motion), 180° shutter, natural motion blur.

## Frame 5 of 6 — IMAGE PROMPT

MAGIC EFFECT — POWER-UP LEVEL-UP BURST (the single impossible move): a radial burst of glowing power-up energy blooms outward from her — concentric rings of bright light, a swirling shower of glowing pixel-sparks and rising light-stars, leveling her up — bright pop game-energy, not fire, not text. PHASE 2 (burst, 0.5–1.8s) — concentric power-up rings expand outward and a swirling shower of glowing pixel-sparks and light-stars rises and fans around her. PHASE 3 (drift, 1.2–2.5s) — the pixel-sparks swirl and drift outward along believable arcs, each grain catching a bright pop specular. PHASE 4 (settle) — a soft sustained halo of slowly falling light-stars rings her, a gentle pop glow pooling around her. KEYFRAME LOCK — START: glow gathering at chest, facets brightening; END: full power-up bloom sustained, pixel-sparks and stars adrift, recognizable face radiant. Only the burst is impossible; gravity, light, drape and skin all behave believably within the stylized world; no gravity-fail, no double-Aira, no blown highlights, no text.

WARDROBE: (resolved) — the voxel digital-couture gown alive with bright facets racing light, every plane glinting, fully modest high neckline and long line; form immaculate with believable weight and a soft sway.

### 🎵 Master Track Brief (External Audio Layer)

MASTER TRACK BRIEF (Gemini Music — paste with the finished 30s silent arcade-voxel reel): Genre — chiptune-cinematic with bright 8-bit synth arpeggios, orchestral strings, a warm pad and a glassy plucked motif; mood — playful rising to triumphant. Because this reel is fully silent (no VO), the track and sound design carry the whole emotional arc — give it a touch more presence and shape than a voiced reel, while still sitting under the SFX and never going full. Tempo — around 128 BPM. Key — C major (bright, upbeat). Arc — 0:00–0:11 a bright 8-bit arpeggio over a warm pad under the opening and the voxel-assembly transform; 0:11–0:20 a string line lifting under the highlighter beat and the wordless Frame-4 player-one look, swelling a little more than usual to fill the silence; 0:20–0:25 a bright chiptune-and-string power-up swell cresting under the level-up burst (its fullest moment, still restrained and never overpowering the SFX); 0:25–0:30 resolving back to the bright arpeggio it began on for a seamless loop. Keep it low and subtle throughout, always under the SFX — a bright playful bed, never a full-volume track. No lyrics, no harsh drops, no genre shifts; loop-safe in and out on C major.

# CONCEPT 66 — CHROME SPEED ATELIER / AERO CARBON-FIBRE COUTURE (SILENT · VEHICLE-TYPE · SUPER CONCEPT)

Title: Pillar 6 — Avant-Garde Sculptural (automotive)

Format: Cinematic Beauty Transformation (AX — vehicle / automotive category) — aero carbon-fibre panel-assembly set transform — fully silent reel · 8 frames (super concept)

Hero Product: "Aero" bold lipstick — a sleek aerodynamic chrome bullet case with a magnetic cap and a deep candy-red bullet

Setting: a sleek automotive design atelier / showroom — a low aerodynamic concept car under dramatic rim light, a mirror-polished floor, brushed-steel walls, soft wind-tunnel vapor drifting low, chrome reflections

Wardrobe: a sleek gunmetal slip (start) → transforms via aero carbon-fibre panel-assembly into an aerodynamic couture gown of lacquered panels, exposed carbon weave and chrome trim

Hair: Sleek Wind-Swept Low Pony with Deep Side Part

Makeup: Chrome Metallic Glow (sculpted luminous skin, chrome-silver lid, crisp contour, glossy candy-red lip)

Movement: a confident reveal → aero panel-assembly transform → product chrome-lipstick swipe hero → a powerful speed-turn → a hero-with-car glamour beat

Concept Line (on-theme, NOT spoken): "engineered to shine"

Duration: 30 seconds | 8 frames

Mood: SLEEK powerful → triumphant

Composition: centred hero against the concept car, chrome reflections, dramatic-but-bright light

Grade: sleek automotive bright — chrome silver, gunmetal, deep candy-red accent, crisp clean whites, controlled rich blacks (cinematic-bright lane, –3, blacks never crushed), no teal-orange

Spoken line: NONE — fully silent reel, no VO; visuals + sleek electronic-orchestral sound design carry it (Frame 4 is a wordless direct-to-lens hero beat)

Audio note: per-frame in-video AUDIO is diegetic-plus-design with no spoken word on any frame; a separate 🎵 Master Track Brief follows the final frame (low and subtle, never full).

Identity safety: the transform locks BOTH keyframes — start = pre-transform recognizable face + gunmetal slip; end = aero carbon-fibre gown, new AND clearly her; the panel-assembly covers only the crossing; only the named transform is impossible, all light, vapor, chrome and fabric obey real physics.

Super-concept note (AV / AZ): this reel runs to 8 frames for an extended automotive arc — arrival, transform, product hero, a wordless look, the magic climax, a powerful speed-turn, a hero-with-car glamour beat, and the loop close.

Visual-lead note (silent reel): with zero dialogue, every beat is built to stop a scroll on image alone — chrome reflections, carbon-weave texture, dramatic rim light, a commanding hero look and a maximal slipstream climax.

## Frame 1 of 8 — IMAGE PROMPT

IDENTITY LOCK: Aira [paste identity-lock reference sheet here].

WARDROBE: a sleek gunmetal silk slip, floor-length and fully modest with a smooth high neckline and a fine gathered shoulder, the metallic silk falling in soft natural folds and catching the rim light in clean silver highlights; a slim tonal tie at the waist; fabric immaculate, weighted and flawless with no creasing.

HAIR (Sleek Wind-Swept Low Pony with Deep Side Part): a deep side part sweeping into a sleek low pony at the nape, mirror-smooth with a clean aerodynamic line and a faint wind-swept lift, fine face-framing pieces; individual strands reading as real hair under a cool silver sheen, hair colour locked to the reference.

MAKEUP (Chrome Metallic Glow): a sculpted luminous base, a chrome-silver wash on the lids, a crisp clean lash, soft brushed-up brows, a crisp contour along the cheekbone, a glossy candy-red lip, inner-corner brightener; dewy real skin with visible pores and fine vellus texture (glow from light, never plastic).

HANDS & NAILS: both hands cradling the chrome lipstick bullet at the collarbone, fingertips light on the polished case with natural nail-bed colour; almond nails in a chrome-pearl gloss, neat cuticles.

FACIAL MICRO-DYNAMICS: a confident composed poise — a knowing closed-lip smile, eyes sharp and direct with a crisp cool catchlight, brows level; the faintest living softness, alive not frozen.

BODY POSTURE & WEIGHT: standing confidently beside the concept car, three-quarter to camera, the lipstick held to the collarbone, weight settled on the back leg with a soft hip tilt, front shoulder dropped, spine long, chin level — living stillness.

KINETIC STILLNESS (8 layers): (1) soft wind-tunnel vapor drifting slowly low across the polished floor; (2) a fine face-framing piece swaying a few millimetres in the moving air; (3) the gunmetal slip breathing with a slow silver-highlight shift as she inhales; (4) a slow chrome reflection sliding along the concept car's flank behind her; (5) chest rising on a soft breath; (6) a cool specular travelling the lipstick case; (7) a rim-light glint crawling the brushed-steel wall; (8) a slow soft blink completing.

SPATIAL LOGIC: FOREGROUND — the chrome lipstick bullet and her cradling hands at the collarbone, tack sharp, case forward. MIDGROUND — Aira beside the concept car, three-quarter torso. BACKGROUND — a low aerodynamic concept car under dramatic rim light, a mirror-polished floor, brushed-steel walls, soft wind-tunnel vapor low, chrome reflections, sculptural depth.

LIGHTING: KEY — a clean bright key softboxed from above-front. FILL — a soft bounce keeping shadows open and bright. RIM — a crisp cool rim raking hair, shoulder and the car's edge. PRACTICAL — the car's sleek light signature glowing low and a soft showroom glow. SEPARATION — a brighter gradient hotspot behind her head. BOUNCE — a soft card adding a clean catch to the lipstick case.

FRAMING: ASPECT 9:16; medium-full, three-quarter, 12% headroom, clean negative space camera-left for the transform to arrive, the car anchoring the lower frame.
SUBJECT FRAMING & POSITION: A medium-full three-quarter hero beside the concept car with about 12% headroom, framed from roughly mid-thigh up. Her torso at a three-quarter angle to camera, weight settled, the product held to the collarbone. Camera at a medium-full establishing distance, negative space camera-left for the transform to arrive. She holds the centre in a poised three-quarter to camera, near-frontal but angled. ORIENTATION LOCK — preserve this exact left-to-right composition; do not mirror, flip or invert the frame.

LENS + DOF + BOKEH: 50mm, f/2.5; her face and the lipstick case tack sharp, the showroom and car melting to a sleek field, soft bokeh off the chrome reflections and vapor; clean highlight roll-off, no clipping, blacks rich not crushed.

COMPOSITION REFERENCE: avant-garde automotive beauty hero (a sleek concept-car campaign still) — chrome, dramatic, refined and phone-stopping.

ATMOSPHERE: sleek, powerful, composed — the still beat before the aero panels assemble her gown.

COLOR GRADE: sleek automotive bright — chrome silver, gunmetal, deep candy-red accent, crisp clean whites, controlled rich blacks, full mid-tone detail (cinematic-bright lane, –3, blacks never crushed), no teal-orange.

TEXTURE & MICRO-DETAIL: the chrome lipstick case shows real polished chrome with a crisp mirror specular; the gunmetal silk shows real metallic fibre and soft folds; dewy real skin micro-texture with peach-fuzz; the concept car shows real lacquer, panel-gaps and carbon-weave accents; wind-tunnel vapor drifts believably low.

NEGATIVE: mirrored, flipped, inverted, extra limbs, fused fingers, melted features, asymmetric eyes (unintentional), garbled text, floating shadows, duplicated subjects, low-res patches, plastic skin, over-smoothed skin, oversharpened, AI-generated artifacts, watermarks, signature, deformed hands, malformed teeth, joint cuts at ankle/knee/wrist/elbow/neck, amputated fingertips, cross-eyed, lazy eye, off-axis pupils, melted clothing, mismatched footwear, mannequin-stiff posture, dead eyes, statue-frozen energy, garbled label text, crushed blacks, frozen vapor.

## Frame 1 of 8 — VIDEO PROMPT

IDENTITY & CHARACTER-CONSISTENCY LOCK: render Aira strictly from the locked identity reference and hold her the SAME PERSON for the entire 6-second clip and every generated frame. Treat identity as a FIXED skeleton that never changes — bone structure (skull, cheekbones, jawline, chin), overall face shape and proportions, eye shape, spacing and iris colour, nose and lip shape, skin tone and real skin texture (visible pores, fine peach-fuzz; no plastic smoothing, no beautification creep, no face re-shaping), hairline, hair colour and texture, height, build and hand/finger anatomy. ONLY the soft expression muscles may move — eyelids, brows, cheeks and mouth corners — together with the eyes, gaze and head; expression and lip-sync animate ON TOP of that fixed identity and must NEVER alter the face shape, the eye colour, or who she is. Keep expression changes smooth and moderate and lead them with the EYES and GAZE (soft smiles, brow lifts, a parted breath, eyes tracing the object) — no extreme grimace, no wide-mouth distortion, no hard squint, no rapid expression flicker that would warp the geometry; favour one clean expression transition per clip. ABSOLUTE ZERO identity drift: no morphing, melting, smearing, rubbery warping, face-swapping, twinning or a second/duplicate subject, no age, weight, ethnicity or gender shift, no extra, missing or fused fingers or limbs. Identity stays identical at the first frame, every in-between frame, the loop seam and the last frame. Through any transform, impossible move or fast action ONLY the intended element (wardrobe, environment, props or effect) changes — her face, identity and proportions stay fully recognizable; interpolate strictly between the locked keyframes and never invent a new face, new hands, extra limbs or a second subject. Do not mirror, flip or invert the frame.

SHOT TYPE: medium-full three-quarter hero beside the concept car.

LENS: 50mm, f/2.5.

SHOT BREAKDOWN (timed, 6s · real-time, continuous energetic motion — never slow-motion, never a static hold; expression eye-led and identity-safe):
- [00:00–00:02] Medium-full three-quarter hero beside the low concept car: a slow push-in is already gliding toward her as she holds the chrome lipstick to her collarbone, the gunmetal slip breathing and wind-tunnel vapor drifting low; she lifts her eyes to lens with a confident composed smile.
- [00:02–00:04] The push continues as she begins a confident quarter-turn, a cool highlight travelling the slip and a chrome reflection sliding along the car; gaze easing to lens, chin level.
- [00:04–00:06] She settles mid-turn, weight rolling onto the front foot, a sleek powerful calm holding as rim-light rakes the car behind.

LIGHTING: clean bright key, soft fill, crisp cool rim on hair and car; the key steady.

ENVIRONMENT: chrome showroom, concept car, polished floor, wind-tunnel vapor low, chrome reflections.

AUDIO: diegetic-plus-design, no spoken word (silent reel). Ambient bed — a low sleek showroom room-tone with a faint deep synth hum and a gentle settle, very low and steady. SFX — a soft case-tap as her fingers settle at 0.6s, a faint silk shift at 3.3s, a cool metallic chime accent on the turn at 3.9s. Reaction — a soft breath at 2.7s. VO — none. The master track enters here soft and ambient (low and subtle, never full).

DURATION: 6 seconds (the clip plays the full 6s at real-time natural speed).

FRAME RATE + MOTION BLUR: 24fps, real-time playback at natural speed (no slow-motion), 180° shutter, natural motion blur.

LIP-SYNC: none — a confident closed-lip smile (silent throughout).

NEGATIVE: morphing, warping, identity drift, limb teleport, object pop-in, frame skip, mirrored composition, text overlay, watermark, AI artifact, ghosting, double-exposure unless intended, lip-sync drift, audio desync, wardrobe drift between shots, unmotivated camera shake, physics violation (heel/hair/chrome), premature transform, mannequin posture, frozen background, identity flicker.

## Frame 2 of 8 — IMAGE PROMPT

IDENTITY LOCK: Aira [paste identity-lock reference sheet here].

TRANSFORM KEYFRAME NOTE: the aero carbon-fibre panel-assembly set transform. START KEYFRAME (locked) — Aira mid-turn in the gunmetal slip, face fully recognizable, caught as the first lacquered aero panel folds in at the hip. CROSSING (the only impossible beat) — sleek lacquered body panels with exposed carbon weave and chrome trim sweep in from the air and fold-and-lock onto her form section by section, assembling an aerodynamic couture gown, panel-gaps sealing with a clean mechanical precision and a thin chrome seam-light tracing each lock. END KEYFRAME (locked) — Aira in the finished aero carbon-fibre gown, the new look fully resolved, new AND unmistakably her. Identity, bone structure and hair colour ride through untouched; the panel-assembly covers only the crossing; gown weight, drape, light and shadow obey real physics on both sides; no double-Aira, no face morph, no melt.

HAIR (Sleek Wind-Swept Low Pony with Deep Side Part): unchanged through the crossing — the deep side part and sleek low pony hold, mirror-smooth, now catching a cool glint from the locking chrome trim; individual strands still real under the silver sheen, hair colour locked to the reference.

MAKEUP (Chrome Metallic Glow): the sculpted base now picking up a cool chrome bounce from the panels, chrome-silver lid and candy-red lip unchanged; skin still real with visible pores and peach-fuzz.

HANDS & NAILS: one hand lifting away as a panel folds up past it, fingers relaxed and clear of the assembly front; almond nails in chrome-pearl gloss, neat cuticles, natural nail-bed colour.

FACIAL MICRO-DYNAMICS: lips parting on a small awed breath as the panels lock, eyes following the gown assembling across her with composed wonder, a brow lifted; cool catchlights; alive and reacting, never frozen.

BODY POSTURE & WEIGHT: caught mid-turn, weight transferring onto the front foot, torso opening toward camera, one arm easing out as the gown assembles in — powerful and balanced, living stillness.

KINETIC STILLNESS (8 layers): (1) a thin chrome seam-light tracing each panel as it locks; (2) wind-tunnel vapor curling off the assembly front in sequence; (3) the slip hem still settling from her turn below the assembly front; (4) a finished panel catching a crisp chrome specular as it seats; (5) a face-framing piece lifting a touch in the moving air; (6) cool speculars travelling the new lacquer; (7) chest rising on the awed breath; (8) a slow chrome reflection sliding on the car behind.

SPATIAL LOGIC: FOREGROUND — the folding panel and the lifted hand, sharp. MIDGROUND — Aira mid-transform, the assembly front dividing slip and panel-gown. BACKGROUND — the showroom and concept car softly blurred, polished floor, wind-tunnel vapor low, dramatic rim light, chrome reflections.

LIGHTING: KEY — clean bright key, now with a crisp cool kick raking the locking panels. FILL — a soft bounce keeping the lower slip readable. RIM — a crisp cool rim on hair and shoulder. PRACTICAL — the car's light signature and showroom glow. SEPARATION — the brighter gradient hotspot behind. BOUNCE — a card keeping the lower slip from going flat.

Her body caught mid-turn, weight transferring onto the front foot, torso opening toward the lens, one arm beginning the transform gesture. Camera low for elegance, the complete figure in frame in the chrome speed atelier, clean negative space. She is centred, caught mid-motion rather than posed. ## Frame 4 of 8 — IMAGE PROMPT (wordless hero beat — no spoken line; visual-led)

IDENTITY LOCK: Aira [paste identity-lock reference sheet here].

VISUAL-LEAD NOTE (silent reel): with no spoken line, this beat lands entirely on image power — a commanding direct-to-lens hero look, sharp eye contact and a single confident gesture; let the chrome reflections, the cool catchlights and the candy-red lip carry the payoff a voice would otherwise deliver.

WARDROBE: (resolved) — the aero carbon-fibre gown of lacquered panels, exposed carbon weave and chrome trim, fully modest high neckline and long line, catching dramatic rim light with crisp chrome speculars; form sitting with real weight.

HAIR (Sleek Wind-Swept Low Pony with Deep Side Part): deep side part sweeping into a sleek low pony, mirror-smooth with a faint wind-swept lift and fine face-framing pieces; real strands under a cool silver sheen, hair colour locked to the reference.

MAKEUP (Chrome Metallic Glow): sculpted luminous base, chrome-silver lid wash, crisp contour, glossy candy-red lip, inner-corner brightener; real skin with visible pores and peach-fuzz.

HANDS & NAILS: the chrome lipstick case held softly at chest height in one hand, label forward, the other hand relaxed open; almond nails in chrome-pearl gloss, neat cuticles, natural nail-bed colour.

FACIAL MICRO-DYNAMICS: direct wordless address to lens — a confident composed smile reaching the eyes, a single assured chin-lift, a slow blink, lips softly closed (no speech); crisp twin cool catchlights firing in the iris; magnetic and alive — the beat lands on the look, not on a line.

BODY POSTURE & WEIGHT: squared a touch more to camera, chin level, spine long, one shoulder dropped, weight settled, the case presented at the chest — a bold hero stance, grounded.

KINETIC STILLNESS (8 layers): (1) a face-framing piece drifting at the cheek; (2) chrome panels glinting on the gown as she breathes; (3) chest rising on a soft breath; (4) wind-tunnel vapor drifting low behind; (5) a slow confident blink completing; (6) a cool specular sliding on the lipstick case; (7) a chrome reflection sliding on the car behind; (8) the faintest lift at the lip-corner settling the smile.

INSTRUMENTATION — deep analog synth-bass, legato string section, a glassy chrome-pluck, a soft pad, a tight metallic tick, no drums beyond the subtle pulse. ARC — cool composed open (F1) → a clean filter-rise under the panel-assembly (F2) → settled and intimate under the lipstick hero and wordless beat (F3–F4) → the fullest-but-still-restrained crest under the slipstream climax (F5) → a low driving pulse under the speed-turn (F6) → easing to composed calm under the hero-with-car beat (F7) → resolving back to the opening ambient phrase for a seamless loop (F8). MIX — sits low and sleek under the engine-design SFX and never competes; supportive, cinematic, automotive. No lyrics, no harsh drops, no genre shifts; loop-safe in and out on G minor.

# CONCEPT 67 — FORGE ATELIER / WROUGHT-IRON FILIGREE COUTURE (SILENT)

Title: Pillar 6 — Avant-Garde Sculptural (artisan metalwork)

Format: Cinematic Beauty Transformation (AX — artisan / craft category) — wrought-iron filigree forge set transform — fully silent reel · 6 frames

Hero Product: "Gilt" cream bronzer — an ornate gold filigree-cased compact with a gilded sculpting brush, a warm bronze-rust cream

Setting: an artisan ironwork forge / metal atelier — warm forge-glow, dark hammered-steel walls, an anvil and scroll-iron forms, fine drifting forge-warm haze, soft gold reflections (bright-warm lane, blacks open)

Wardrobe: a fitted black silk slip (start) → transforms via wrought-iron filigree forge into an ornate couture gown of black iron scrollwork laced with gold-leaf highlights

Hair: Sculpted Low Chignon with Filigree-Waved Tendrils

Makeup: Warm Gilded Glow (bronzed sculpted skin, soft gold lid, deep rust lip)

Movement: a composed reveal → wrought-iron filigree transform → product gilded-bronzer sweep hero → a wordless look → molten-gold filigree-light bloom climax → loop close

Concept Line (on-theme, NOT spoken): "wrought by hand"

Duration: 30 seconds | 6 frames

Mood: WARM crafted → radiant

Composition: centred hero against the forge, gold reflections, dramatic-but-bright warm light

Grade: bright-warm artisan — deep iron-black, warm gold, bronze-rust accent, crisp warm whites, controlled rich blacks (cinematic-bright lane, –3, blacks never crushed), no teal-orange

Spoken line: NONE — fully silent reel, no VO; visuals + warm orchestral-forge sound design carry it (Frame 4 is a wordless direct-to-lens hero beat)

Audio note: per-frame in-video AUDIO is diegetic-plus-design with no spoken word on any frame; a separate 🎵 Master Track Brief follows the final frame (low and subtle, never full).

Identity safety: the transform locks BOTH keyframes — start = pre-transform recognizable face + black slip; end = wrought-iron filigree gown, new AND clearly her; the scrollwork-weave covers only the crossing; only the named transform is impossible, all light, heat-shimmer, gold and fabric obey real physics.

Visual-lead note (silent reel): with zero dialogue, every beat is built to stop a scroll on image alone — glowing iron scrollwork, gold-leaf highlights, warm forge light, a commanding hero look and a maximal molten-gold climax.

## Frame 6 of 6 — VIDEO PROMPT (LOOP CLOSE)

IDENTITY & CHARACTER-CONSISTENCY LOCK: render Aira strictly from the locked identity reference and hold her the SAME PERSON for the entire 6-second clip and every generated frame. Treat identity as a FIXED skeleton that never changes — bone structure (skull, cheekbones, jawline, chin), overall face shape and proportions, eye shape, spacing and iris colour, nose and lip shape, skin tone and real skin texture (visible pores, fine peach-fuzz; no plastic smoothing, no beautification creep, no face re-shaping), hairline, hair colour and texture, height, build and hand/finger anatomy. ONLY the soft expression muscles may move — eyelids, brows, cheeks and mouth corners — together with the eyes, gaze and head; expression and lip-sync animate ON TOP of that fixed identity and must NEVER alter the face shape, the eye colour, or who she is. Keep expression changes smooth and moderate and lead them with the EYES and GAZE (soft smiles, brow lifts, a parted breath, eyes tracing the object) — no extreme grimace, no wide-mouth distortion, no hard squint, no rapid expression flicker that would warp the geometry; favour one clean expression transition per clip. ABSOLUTE ZERO identity drift: no morphing, melting, smearing, rubbery warping, face-swapping, twinning or a second/duplicate subject, no age, weight, ethnicity or gender shift, no extra, missing or fused fingers or limbs. Identity stays identical at the first frame, every in-between frame, the loop seam and the last frame. Through any transform, impossible move or fast action ONLY the intended element (wardrobe, environment, props or effect) changes — her face, identity and proportions stay fully recognizable; interpolate strictly between the locked keyframes and never invent a new face, new hands, extra limbs or a second subject. Do not mirror, flip or invert the frame.

SHOT TYPE: medium-full three-quarter hero in the forge, matched to Frame 1.

LENS: 50mm, f/2.5.

- [00:02–00:04] The settle continues as the filigree gown eases back toward the black silk's stillness, forge-haze easing and forge-glow pulsing on the wall; a composed knowing smile returning.
- [00:04–00:06] She lands the composed smile and a slow blink, resolving to exactly match the Frame 1 pose, weight settled in the forge — a seamless loop seam.

### 🎵 Master Track Brief (External Audio Layer)

A separate, subtle background music bed for the whole 30s reel — mixed low under the per-frame sound design, never full-volume, no vocals. A warm orchestral-forge hybrid in E♭ major, around 72 BPM — a low warm cello-and-double-bass foundation under sustained strings, a soft brass swell and a fine warm anvil-tick, with a gentle rise under the transform (0:05–0:11) and a controlled crest under the molten-gold filigree-light climax (0:20–0:25) before easing back. INSTRUMENTATION — warm cello, double-bass, legato strings, a soft brass swell, a distant choir-pad, a fine metallic anvil-tick, no drums beyond the subtle pulse. ARC — warm composed open (F1) → a gentle string-and-brass rise under the iron-filigree forge (F2) → settled and intimate under the bronzer hero and wordless beat (F3–F4) → the fullest-but-still-restrained crest under the gold-light climax (F5) → resolving back to the opening ambient phrase for a seamless loop (F6). MIX — sits low and warm under the forge-design SFX and never competes; supportive, cinematic, artisan. No lyrics, no harsh drops, no genre shifts; loop-safe in and out on E♭ major.

# CONCEPT 68 — PAPERCRAFT ATELIER / PAPER-QUILLING COUTURE (SILENT)

Title: Pillar 3 — Whimsical Craft

Format: Cinematic Beauty Transformation (AX — papercraft / stationery category) — paper-quilling set transform — fully silent reel · 6 frames

Hero Product: "Petal" cream blush cushion — a pastel paper-wrapped compact with a soft cushion puff, a pastel-pink cream tint

Setting: a bright sunlit papercraft / stationery atelier — soft pastel walls, rolls of coloured paper strips, clean white worktable, fine drifting paper-confetti motes, soft daylight (bright lane, –3)

Wardrobe: a cream silk slip (start) → transforms via paper-quilling into an intricate couture gown of coiled pastel paper strips

Hair: Soft Voluminous Half-Up with Ribboned Paper-Curl Curls

Makeup: Fresh Pastel Glow (dewy skin, soft pastel-pink wash, glossy petal-pink lip)

Movement: a soft reveal → paper-quilling transform → product cushion-blush press hero → a wordless look → paper-bloom unfurl climax → loop close

Concept Line (on-theme, NOT spoken): "folded by hand"

Duration: 30 seconds | 6 frames

Mood: SOFT whimsical → joyful

Composition: centred hero against the pastel atelier, soft daylight, bright airy light

Grade: bright pastel papercraft — soft blush-pink, cream, butter-yellow, mint, lavender, clean white, controlled gentle blacks (cinematic-bright lane, –3), no teal-orange

Spoken line: NONE — fully silent reel, no VO; visuals + light whimsical sound design carry it (Frame 4 is a wordless direct-to-lens hero beat)

Audio note: per-frame in-video AUDIO is diegetic-plus-design with no spoken word on any frame; a separate 🎵 Master Track Brief follows the final frame (low and subtle, never full).

Identity safety: the transform locks BOTH keyframes — start = pre-transform recognizable face + cream slip; end = quilled-paper gown, new AND clearly her; the paper-coil assembly covers only the crossing; only the named transform is impossible, all light, paper, motes and fabric obey real physics.

Visual-lead note (silent reel): with zero dialogue, every beat is built to stop a scroll on image alone — intricate coiled-paper texture, pastel colour, soft daylight, a charming hero look and a maximal paper-bloom climax.

## Frame 6 of 6 — VIDEO PROMPT (LOOP CLOSE)

IDENTITY & CHARACTER-CONSISTENCY LOCK: render Aira strictly from the locked identity reference and hold her the SAME PERSON for the entire 6-second clip and every generated frame. Treat identity as a FIXED skeleton that never changes — bone structure (skull, cheekbones, jawline, chin), overall face shape and proportions, eye shape, spacing and iris colour, nose and lip shape, skin tone and real skin texture (visible pores, fine peach-fuzz; no plastic smoothing, no beautification creep, no face re-shaping), hairline, hair colour and texture, height, build and hand/finger anatomy. ONLY the soft expression muscles may move — eyelids, brows, cheeks and mouth corners — together with the eyes, gaze and head; expression and lip-sync animate ON TOP of that fixed identity and must NEVER alter the face shape, the eye colour, or who she is. Keep expression changes smooth and moderate and lead them with the EYES and GAZE (soft smiles, brow lifts, a parted breath, eyes tracing the object) — no extreme grimace, no wide-mouth distortion, no hard squint, no rapid expression flicker that would warp the geometry; favour one clean expression transition per clip. ABSOLUTE ZERO identity drift: no morphing, melting, smearing, rubbery warping, face-swapping, twinning or a second/duplicate subject, no age, weight, ethnicity or gender shift, no extra, missing or fused fingers or limbs. Identity stays identical at the first frame, every in-between frame, the loop seam and the last frame. Through any transform, impossible move or fast action ONLY the intended element (wardrobe, environment, props or effect) changes — her face, identity and proportions stay fully recognizable; interpolate strictly between the locked keyframes and never invent a new face, new hands, extra limbs or a second subject. Do not mirror, flip or invert the frame.

SHOT TYPE: medium-full three-quarter hero in the atelier, matched to Frame 1.

### 🎵 Master Track Brief (External Audio Layer)

A separate, subtle background music bed for the whole 30s reel — mixed low under the per-frame sound design, never full-volume, no vocals. A light whimsical-orchestral piece in B major, around 100 BPM — a gentle pizzicato-string pulse under a soft glockenspiel-and-celesta melody, a light harp shimmer and a soft string-pad, with a gentle lift under the transform (0:05–0:11) and a controlled crest under the paper-bloom unfurl climax (0:20–0:25) before easing back. INSTRUMENTATION — pizzicato strings, glockenspiel, celesta, harp, a soft string-pad, a light wood-block tick, no drums beyond the gentle pulse. ARC — soft charming open (F1) → a gentle pizzicato-and-celesta lift under the paper-quilling (F2) → settled and intimate under the cushion-blush hero and wordless beat (F3–F4) → the fullest-but-still-restrained crest under the paper-bloom climax (F5) → resolving back to the opening ambient phrase for a seamless loop (F6). MIX — sits low and light under the papercraft-design SFX and never competes; supportive, whimsical, charming. No lyrics, no harsh drops, no genre shifts; loop-safe in and out on B major.

# CONCEPT 69 — INK ATELIER / SMOKE-CALLIGRAPHY COUTURE (SILENT)

Title: Pillar 2 — Editorial Bold

Format: Cinematic Beauty Transformation (AX — ink / calligraphy category) — smoke-calligraphy set transform — fully silent reel · 6 frames

Hero Product: "Inkstroke" liquid liner — a sleek black-and-silver lacquer pen with a fine calligraphy brush tip, deep ink-black

Setting: a dark ink-and-calligraphy atelier / sumi-e studio — deep charcoal walls, a single warm lamp, hanging ink-wash scrolls, a stone inkstone and brushes, fine ink-smoke drifting, controlled rich blacks (dramatic dark-elegant lane, blacks never crushed)

Wardrobe: a black silk slip (start) → transforms via smoke-calligraphy into a flowing gown of inky calligraphic strokes and smoke-silk with fine silver-ink edges

Hair: Sleek Centre-Parted Low Knot with a Single Ink-Black Lacquered Wave

Makeup: Graphic Ink Eye (sharp black winged liner, soft-smoked outer, porcelain skin, deep berry-stain lip)

Movement: a poised reveal → smoke-calligraphy transform → product liner wing-flick hero → a wordless look → silver ink-bloom climax → loop close

Concept Line (on-theme, NOT spoken): "in a single stroke"

Duration: 30 seconds | 6 frames

Mood: DRAMATIC → elegant

Composition: centred hero against the dark atelier, single warm lamp, controlled chiaroscuro

Grade: dramatic dark-elegant ink — deep charcoal, ink-black, warm lamp-gold accent, cool silver-ink, porcelain skin, rich blacks never crushed (controlled chiaroscuro lane), no teal-orange

Spoken line: NONE — fully silent reel, no VO; visuals + spare elegant sound design carry it (Frame 4 is a wordless direct-to-lens hero beat)

Audio note: per-frame in-video AUDIO is diegetic-plus-design with no spoken word on any frame; a separate 🎵 Master Track Brief follows the final frame (low and subtle, never full).

Identity safety: the transform locks BOTH keyframes — start = pre-transform recognizable face + black slip; end = smoke-calligraphy gown, new AND clearly her; the ink-smoke draw covers only the crossing; only the named transform is impossible, all light, ink, smoke and fabric obey real physics.

Visual-lead note (silent reel): with zero dialogue, every beat is built to stop a scroll on image alone — dramatic chiaroscuro, flowing ink-stroke texture, porcelain skin against deep black, a commanding hero look and a maximal silver ink-bloom climax.

Tonal-lane note: deliberately a DARK dramatic lane (deep charcoal + warm lamp + silver) to contrast the bright reels around it — blacks stay rich but never crushed, skin stays luminous and real.

LIGHTING: warm lamp key with a cool kick on the drawing strokes, low soft fill, cool silver rim; dramatic and steady.

ENVIRONMENT: ink atelier, hanging scrolls, the warm lamp, drifting ink-smoke, charcoal walls.

AUDIO: diegetic-plus-design, no spoken word (silent reel). Ambient bed — the deep atelier tone with a soft low cello-and-koto rise under the crossing. SFX — a soft ink-stream whoosh 0.0–1.5s, a delicate sequence of soft brush-stroke sweeps and fine ink-settle taps as the gown draws 1.5–4.2s, a soft resolve-tone as the gown sets at 5.4s, faint smoke-wisp drift off the front. Reaction — a soft held breath at 1.4s. VO — none. Master track lifts gently under the transform then eases (subtle, never full).

DURATION: 6 seconds (0:05–0:11 of 30s total).

## Frame 3 of 6 — VIDEO PROMPT

PHASE 3 (drift, 1.2–2.5s) — the silver ink-motes swirl and trail outward along real air-currents, each grain catching a cool specular. PHASE 4 (settle) — a soft sustained silver ink-glow rings her, a gentle cool light pooling around her. FACIAL MICRO-DYNAMICS: the poised knowing set resettling to match the opening, eyes direct and luminous with a crisp warm catchlight, brows level; the faintest living softness, alive not frozen (no speech).

BODY POSTURE & WEIGHT: returned to the opening stance in the atelier, three-quarter to camera, pen at the collarbone, weight on the back leg with a soft hip tilt, spine long, chin level — living stillness matching Frame 1.

KINETIC STILLNESS (8 layers): (1) fine ink-smoke drifting slowly up-frame through the lamp-beam, easing to the opening rhythm; (2) the single lacquered wave settling at the temple; (3) the gown breathing with a slow sheen shift as she inhales; (4) a hanging ink-wash scroll swaying a hair behind; (5) chest rising on a soft breath; (6) a soft specular settling on the silver pen-clip; (7) the warm lamp glowing gently against the charcoal wall; (8) a slow soft blink completing, matching the opening beat.

SPATIAL LOGIC: FOREGROUND — the black-and-silver liner pen and her cradling hands at the collarbone, tack sharp, silver clip forward. MIDGROUND — Aira poised in the atelier, three-quarter torso. BACKGROUND — deep charcoal walls, a single warm lamp, hanging ink-wash scrolls, a stone inkstone and brushes, fine drifting ink-smoke — matching Frame 1.

LIGHTING: KEY — a single warm lamp keyed from above-front as a soft directional beauty key, matched to Frame 1. FILL — a low soft bounce keeping the shadow side readable. RIM — a cool silver rim skimming hair and shoulder. PRACTICAL — the warm lamp in shot. SEPARATION — a faint warm gradient halo behind her head. BOUNCE — a soft card adding a clean catch to the pen.

FRAMING: ASPECT 9:16; medium-full, three-quarter, 12% headroom, the inkstone anchoring the lower frame, clean negative space camera-left — matched to Frame 1 for the loop.
SUBJECT FRAMING & POSITION: A medium-full three-quarter hero in the ink atelier with about 12% headroom, framed from roughly mid-thigh up. Her torso at a three-quarter angle to camera, weight settled, the product held to the collarbone. Returning to the exact opening setup, this recovers Frame 1's composition for a seamless loop. ATMOSPHERE: poised, dramatic, resolved — the reel landing exactly where it began, now transformed.

## Frame 6 of 6 — VIDEO PROMPT (LOOP CLOSE)

IDENTITY & CHARACTER-CONSISTENCY LOCK: render Aira strictly from the locked identity reference and hold her the SAME PERSON for the entire 6-second clip and every generated frame. Treat identity as a FIXED skeleton that never changes — bone structure (skull, cheekbones, jawline, chin), overall face shape and proportions, eye shape, spacing and iris colour, nose and lip shape, skin tone and real skin texture (visible pores, fine peach-fuzz; no plastic smoothing, no beautification creep, no face re-shaping), hairline, hair colour and texture, height, build and hand/finger anatomy. ONLY the soft expression muscles may move — eyelids, brows, cheeks and mouth corners — together with the eyes, gaze and head; expression and lip-sync animate ON TOP of that fixed identity and must NEVER alter the face shape, the eye colour, or who she is. Keep expression changes smooth and moderate and lead them with the EYES and GAZE (soft smiles, brow lifts, a parted breath, eyes tracing the object) — no extreme grimace, no wide-mouth distortion, no hard squint, no rapid expression flicker that would warp the geometry; favour one clean expression transition per clip. ABSOLUTE ZERO identity drift: no morphing, melting, smearing, rubbery warping, face-swapping, twinning or a second/duplicate subject, no age, weight, ethnicity or gender shift, no extra, missing or fused fingers or limbs. Identity stays identical at the first frame, every in-between frame, the loop seam and the last frame. Through any transform, impossible move or fast action ONLY the intended element (wardrobe, environment, props or effect) changes — her face, identity and proportions stay fully recognizable; interpolate strictly between the locked keyframes and never invent a new face, new hands, extra limbs or a second subject. Do not mirror, flip or invert the frame.

SHOT TYPE: medium-full three-quarter hero in the ink atelier, matched to Frame 1.

CAMERA MOVEMENT: a slow settle to rest matching the Frame 1 push so the end frame meets the start frame cleanly — one move, loop-safe.

LENS: 50mm, f/2.5.

SUBJECT ACTION WITH TIMING:
- 0.0–3.0s — Aira eases back to the opening stance, cradling the liner pen at the collarbone, the gown re-settling.
- 3.0–6.0s — she lands the poised knowing set and a slow blink, ink-smoke drifting, the warm lamp glowing on the wall — the frame resolving to exactly match Frame 1 for a seamless loop. Real physics throughout.

LIGHTING: single warm lamp key, low soft fill, cool silver rim — matched to Frame 1; steady, no flicker.

ENVIRONMENT: ink atelier, hanging scrolls, inkstone and brushes, drifting ink-smoke, charcoal walls — matched to the opening.

AUDIO: diegetic-plus-design, no spoken word (silent reel). Ambient bed — the deep quiet atelier room-tone with a faint warm lamp-hush, easing back to exactly the Frame 1 level for a seamless audio loop. SFX — a soft lacquer-pen settle as her hands return at 1.0s, a faint silk shift at 3.1s, a single soft koto-pluck resettling at 3.6s matched to the opening accent. Reaction — a soft breath at 2.6s. VO — none. Master track resolves softly back to its opening ambient phrase for a clean loop (subtle, never full).

DURATION: 5 seconds (0:25–0:30 of 30s total).

FRAME RATE + MOTION BLUR: 24fps, 180° shutter, gentle natural motion blur easing to the matched settle.

LIP-SYNC: none — a poised closed-lip set (silent).

NEGATIVE: morphing, warping, identity drift, limb teleport, object pop-in, frame skip, mirrored composition, text overlay, watermark, AI artifact, ghosting, lip-sync drift, audio desync, wardrobe drift between shots, unmotivated camera shake, physics violation (glow/silver/fabric), loop mismatch, mannequin posture, frozen background, identity flicker.

### 🎵 Master Track Brief (External Audio Layer)

A separate, subtle background music bed for the whole 30s reel — mixed low under the per-frame sound design, never full-volume, no vocals. A dark cinematic-elegant piece in C minor, around 88 BPM — a low warm cello-and-double-bass foundation under a sparse low piano, a single resonant koto/guzheng pluck motif and a soft taiko-brush pulse, with a gentle rise under the transform (0:05–0:11) and a controlled crest under the silver ink-bloom climax (0:20–0:25) before easing back. INSTRUMENTATION — cello, double-bass, sparse low piano, koto/guzheng, a soft string-pad, a brushed taiko, a faint metallic shimmer, no drums beyond the soft brushed pulse. ARC — poised dramatic open (F1) → a low cello-and-koto rise under the smoke-calligraphy (F2) → settled and intimate under the liner hero and wordless beat (F3–F4) → the fullest-but-still-restrained crest under the silver ink-bloom climax (F5) → resolving back to the opening ambient phrase for a seamless loop (F6). MIX — sits low and dark under the ink-design SFX and never competes; supportive, cinematic, elegant. No lyrics, no harsh drops, no genre shifts; loop-safe in and out on C minor.

# CONCEPT 70 — GILDED BALLROOM / CRYSTAL-CHANDELIER COUTURE (SILENT — GRAND FINALE)

Title: Pillar 4 — Opulent Jewel-Tone (grand finale)

Format: Cinematic Beauty Transformation — crystal-chandelier drop-assembly transform (silent)

Hero Product: "Éclat" crystal-glow drops — a faceted cut-crystal dropper vial shaped like a chandelier droplet, with a warm champagne-pearl liquid and a slim gold collar

Setting: a vast gilded ballroom at golden hour — towering crystal chandeliers, tall gilded mirrors, a pale marble floor, warm candlelight, drifting golden light-motes

Wardrobe: a champagne silk-satin column gown (start) → transforms via crystal-chandelier drop-assembly into a luminous crystal-drop couture gown of strung faceted cut-crystal droplets over champagne silk

Hair: Sculpted Side-Swept Chignon with a soft centre part

Makeup: Luminous Champagne-Glow (lit champagne highlight, soft rose-gold lip)

Movement: a slow regal turn → crystal-chandelier drop-assembly transform → product crystal-glow hero → wordless grand hero beat

Concept Line: "lit by a thousand crystals" (on-theme, NOT spoken)

Duration: 30 seconds | 6 frames

Mood: REGAL → dazzling

Composition: symmetrical gilded ballroom, towering chandeliers framing, warm candlelit pools on marble

Grade: luminous warm-gold opulent — warm champagne and candle-gold, gilded highlights, soft prismatic sparkle, porcelain skin, rich-but-airy blacks (cinematic lane)

Spoken line: NONE — fully silent reel; no VO or spoken line on any frame.

Audio note: per-frame in-video AUDIO is diegetic-plus-design with NO spoken word; a separate 🎵 Master Track Brief follows the final frame (low and subtle, never full).

Identity safety: the transform locks BOTH keyframes — start = pre-transform recognizable face + champagne silk gown; end = crystal-drop couture gown, new AND clearly her; the drop-assembly covers only the crossing; only the named transform is impossible, all light, crystal and fabric obey real physics.

Visual-lead note (silent): with zero dialogue, every beat lands on image and motion — a regal turn, a dazzling crystal cascade, refracted candlelight and a commanding wordless look carry the whole story; sound design and the master track support, never speak.

Grand-finale note: this is the closing concept of the 20-reel set — pitched as the most opulent and luminous, a bright warm-gold ballroom crescendo set in deliberate contrast to Concept 69's dark ink chiaroscuro, built to end the series on a dazzling high.

## Frame 3 of 6 — VIDEO PROMPT

IDENTITY & CHARACTER-CONSISTENCY LOCK: render Aira strictly from the locked identity reference and hold her the SAME PERSON for the entire 6-second clip and every generated frame. Treat identity as a FIXED skeleton that never changes — bone structure (skull, cheekbones, jawline, chin), overall face shape and proportions, eye shape, spacing and iris colour, nose and lip shape, skin tone and real skin texture (visible pores, fine peach-fuzz; no plastic smoothing, no beautification creep, no face re-shaping), hairline, hair colour and texture, height, build and hand/finger anatomy. ONLY the soft expression muscles may move — eyelids, brows, cheeks and mouth corners — together with the eyes, gaze and head; expression and lip-sync animate ON TOP of that fixed identity and must NEVER alter the face shape, the eye colour, or who she is. Keep expression changes smooth and moderate and lead them with the EYES and GAZE (soft smiles, brow lifts, a parted breath, eyes tracing the object) — no extreme grimace, no wide-mouth distortion, no hard squint, no rapid expression flicker that would warp the geometry; favour one clean expression transition per clip. ABSOLUTE ZERO identity drift: no morphing, melting, smearing, rubbery warping, face-swapping, twinning or a second/duplicate subject, no age, weight, ethnicity or gender shift, no extra, missing or fused fingers or limbs. Identity stays identical at the first frame, every in-between frame, the loop seam and the last frame. Through any transform, impossible move or fast action ONLY the intended element (wardrobe, environment, props or effect) changes — her face, identity and proportions stay fully recognizable; interpolate strictly between the locked keyframes and never invent a new face, new hands, extra limbs or a second subject. Do not mirror, flip or invert the frame.

## Frame 5 of 6 — IMAGE PROMPT

IDENTITY LOCK: Aira [paste identity-lock reference sheet here].

VISUAL-LEAD NOTE (silent reel): this is the grand climax of the whole 20-reel set — push the spectacle to its peak: a full-frame prismatic rainbow-bloom as a thousand crystals refract the candlelight, drifting rainbow-and-gold light-motes lifting and a commanding hero silhouette, all carried on image and lush sound design with zero dialogue.

MAGIC EFFECT — PRISMATIC CANDLELIGHT RAINBOW-BLOOM (the single impossible move): the gown's thousand faceted crystals catch the candlelight and refract it into drifting rainbow caustics that bloom outward around her into a luminous rainbow-and-gold halo — refracted light, not fire. PHASE 1 (kindle, 0.0–0.5s) — a single crystal at the heart of the bodice splits the candlelight into a first soft rainbow glint. PHASE 2 (bloom, 0.5–1.8s) — the rainbow caustics ripple outward crystal to crystal, every droplet throwing drifting bands of prismatic colour across the marble and the gilded mirrors, the chandeliers blazing brighter in sequence. PHASE 3 (drift, 1.2–2.5s) — fine rainbow-and-gold light-motes lift and swirl outward along real air-currents, each catching a pin-specular. PHASE 4 (settle) — a soft sustained rainbow-and-gold glow rings her, a gentle warm-prismatic light pooling on the marble. KEYFRAME LOCK — START: a single crystal kindling a rainbow glint; END: full prismatic rainbow-bloom sustained, light-motes adrift, recognizable face radiant. Only the prismatic bloom is impossible; gravity, light, drape, shadow and skin all obey real physics; no gravity-fail, no double-Aira, no blown highlights, no fire.

WARDROBE: (resolved) — the crystal-drop gown alive with refracted rainbow caustics dancing across every droplet, the champagne silk glowing warm beneath, fully modest high neckline and long line; form immaculate with real weight and a soft sway.

BODY POSTURE & WEIGHT: a slight powerful back-lean as the bloom crests, chest open, spine long, weight grounded on both feet, arms easing outward — a commanding grand hero silhouette, triumphant and radiant.

KINETIC STILLNESS (8 layers): (1) rainbow caustics rippling outward crystal to crystal; (2) fine rainbow-and-gold light-motes lifting and swirling outward; (3) the gilded mirrors throwing the rainbow back in travelling sequence; (4) fine hair-pieces lifting in the warm air; (5) chest rising on the powerful breath; (6) prismatic speculars travelling the blazing crystals; (7) soft rainbow light dappling across the marble floor; (8) a gentle warm-prismatic glow pooling at her feet.

BACKGROUND — the ballroom alive with rainbow-and-gold light, chandeliers blazing, gilded mirrors multiplying the glow, a deep depth receding.

LIGHTING: KEY — the prismatic rainbow-bloom lifted as a live key across the gown, over the warm candlelight. FILL — bounce from the lit marble. RIM — a warm backlight holding her silhouette inside the swirl. PRACTICAL — the chandeliers and the blazing crystals. SEPARATION — the swirling rainbow halo framing her. BOUNCE — a card sustaining skin and crystal.

## Frame 6 of 6 — VIDEO PROMPT

IDENTITY & CHARACTER-CONSISTENCY LOCK: render Aira strictly from the locked identity reference and hold her the SAME PERSON for the entire 6-second clip and every generated frame. Treat identity as a FIXED skeleton that never changes — bone structure (skull, cheekbones, jawline, chin), overall face shape and proportions, eye shape, spacing and iris colour, nose and lip shape, skin tone and real skin texture (visible pores, fine peach-fuzz; no plastic smoothing, no beautification creep, no face re-shaping), hairline, hair colour and texture, height, build and hand/finger anatomy. ONLY the soft expression muscles may move — eyelids, brows, cheeks and mouth corners — together with the eyes, gaze and head; expression and lip-sync animate ON TOP of that fixed identity and must NEVER alter the face shape, the eye colour, or who she is. Keep expression changes smooth and moderate and lead them with the EYES and GAZE (soft smiles, brow lifts, a parted breath, eyes tracing the object) — no extreme grimace, no wide-mouth distortion, no hard squint, no rapid expression flicker that would warp the geometry; favour one clean expression transition per clip. ABSOLUTE ZERO identity drift: no morphing, melting, smearing, rubbery warping, face-swapping, twinning or a second/duplicate subject, no age, weight, ethnicity or gender shift, no extra, missing or fused fingers or limbs. Identity stays identical at the first frame, every in-between frame, the loop seam and the last frame. Through any transform, impossible move or fast action ONLY the intended element (wardrobe, environment, props or effect) changes — her face, identity and proportions stay fully recognizable; interpolate strictly between the locked keyframes and never invent a new face, new hands, extra limbs or a second subject. Do not mirror, flip or invert the frame.

SHOT TYPE: full-figure resolved hero close, easing toward the F1 opening framing.

CAMERA MOVEMENT: a slow settle back to the F1 start position (gentle reverse of the F1 push) — one smooth move landing exactly on the opening framing for a seamless loop.

LENS: 50mm, f/2.0.

SUBJECT ACTION WITH TIMING:
- 0.0–1.8s — the rainbow-bloom eases out, the last motes drifting down as Aira begins settling back toward her opening posture.
- 1.8–4.2s — she cradles the vial at the collarbone, a soft serene smile easing in, the ballroom resolving to its calm warm look.
- 4.2–6.0s — she lands exactly on the F1 mid-turn pose, expression and framing matched for the loop point. Real physics throughout.

LIGHTING: warm candle key settling to the F1 direction and intensity, marble fill, gold rim; calm and steady, matched at the cut.

ENVIRONMENT: the gilded ballroom resolving to the F1 look — calm chandeliers, candlelight on marble, a few last motes.

AUDIO: diegetic-plus-design, no spoken word (silent reel). Ambient bed — the warm grand-room tone easing back to the calm opening level. SFX — a soft fading crystal shimmer 0.0–1.8s, a gentle vial-settle touch at 2.4s, a final soft sparkle resolving at 4.3s to match the F1 opening texture for the loop. Reaction — a soft settling breath at 2.6s. VO — none. Master track resolves its cadence and eases to the opening ambience for a seamless loop (subtle, never full).

DURATION: 5 seconds (0:25–0:30 of 30s total).

FRAME RATE + MOTION BLUR: 24fps, 180° shutter, gentle natural motion blur settling to the matched opening.

LIP-SYNC: none — a soft serene closing smile, no words (silent).

NEGATIVE: morphing, warping, identity drift, limb teleport, object pop-in, frame skip, mirrored composition, text overlay, watermark, AI artifact, ghosting, lip-sync drift, audio desync, wardrobe drift between shots, unmotivated camera shake, physics violation (glow/crystal/fabric), loop-mismatch, jarring cut, mannequin posture, frozen background, identity flicker.

### 🎵 Master Track Brief (External Audio Layer)

A grand finale orchestral waltz — the dazzling close of the 20-reel set. Key D♭ major, around 78 BPM, in a sweeping 3/4 waltz metre. Instrumentation: warm legato strings carrying the waltz melody, harp arpeggio sweeps, celesta and glockenspiel sparkle accents, a soft timpani swell under the transform and the rainbow-bloom, gentle woodwind countermelody, and a warm low cello-and-bass foundation — no vocals, no lyrics (this is a silent reel; the track is an external layer only). Arc: a soft harp-and-celesta shimmer opening under Frame 1, the waltz lifting gently as the crystal-chandelier drop-assembly strings the gown in Frame 2, an intimate restrained sway under the crystal-glow product hero in Frame 3, a confident swell under the wordless grand hero beat in Frame 4, the fullest-but-still-restrained orchestral-waltz crest with the timpani swell under the prismatic candlelight rainbow-bloom in Frame 5, then resolving its cadence and easing back to the opening harp shimmer for a seamless loop in Frame 6. Mood: opulent, magical, triumphant, elegant. Mix subtle and cinematic, sitting under the per-frame sound design — present but never overpowering, and never a full-foreground music bed.
